gene_description: Phosphatidylethanolamine N-methyltransferase (PEMT) — catalyzes endogenous synthesis of phosphatidylcholine from phosphatidylethanolamine, primary source of choline for liver and brain

source.title: Genetic variants in phosphatidylethanolamine N-methyltransferase (PEMT) and methylenetetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase (MTHFD1) influence biomarkers of choline metabolism when folate intake is restricted
source.finding: rs12325817 variant allele associated with reduced estrogen-induced PEMT expression. 40-75% of women carry this polymorphism. Two copies increase choline deficiency organ dysfunction risk to 80% on low-choline diet vs 13% in non-carriers.

GG.effect: Homozygous for variant G allele. Significantly reduced PEMT expression and choline synthesis capacity, particularly in response to estrogen signaling. Premenopausal women show 80% risk of organ dysfunction (fatty liver, muscle damage) on low-choline diet. Reduced phosphatidylcholine synthesis increases VLDL lipid export demands.
GG.recommendation: Higher dietary choline intake essential, especially for premenopausal women. Target 550 mg/day for women and 550-625 mg/day for men. Include choline-rich foods (eggs, liver, fish, nuts, seeds, Brussels sprouts) or consider supplementation. Monitor liver function with adequate choline intake.

AG.effect: Heterozygous for variant G/wild-type A alleles. Intermediate PEMT expression and choline synthesis capacity. Premenopausal women show ~43% risk of organ dysfunction on low-choline diet. Some reduction in estrogen-responsive choline synthesis.
AG.recommendation: Adequate dietary choline intake recommended (425-550 mg/day depending on sex). Include regular dietary sources of choline. Monitor for signs of choline deficiency (muscle pain, fatty liver) especially on restrictive diets.

GA.effect: Heterozygous for variant G/wild-type A alleles. Intermediate PEMT expression and choline synthesis capacity. Premenopausal women show ~43% risk of organ dysfunction on low-choline diet. Some reduction in estrogen-responsive choline synthesis.
GA.recommendation: Adequate dietary choline intake recommended (425-550 mg/day depending on sex). Include regular dietary sources of choline. Monitor for signs of choline deficiency (muscle pain, fatty liver) especially on restrictive diets.

AA.effect: Homozygous for wild-type A allele. Normal PEMT expression and capacity for endogenous choline synthesis, particularly responsive to estrogen in women. Low risk (13%) of organ dysfunction even on low-choline diet. Adequate de novo choline production from dietary folate and methionine.
AA.recommendation: Standard dietary choline intake sufficient (425-550 mg/day). No special supplementation needed unless on very restricted diet. Regular balanced diet with diverse nutrient sources adequate.
